data.title: Knowledge Infrastructure for the AI Era
data.metaDescription: Trail is a knowledge infrastructure engine that turns your sources into a persistent, compounding Neuron-graph — maintained by an LLM on your behalf. Realizing Vannevar Bush's 1945 Memex vision with modern LLMs.

sections.description: A minimalist engine to ingest, connect, and retrieve your organization's collective intelligence. Reimagining Vannevar Bush's 1945 vision with geometric precision.

sections.title: Architecture of thought.
sections.description: The primitives for a persistent graph of cross-referenced Neurons — compiled by an LLM, curated by you, navigated by association.

items.title: LLM-Compiled Wiki
items.description: Sources aren't chunked and indexed — they're read by an LLM that authors a persistent graph of Neurons on your behalf. Every new fact updates existing Neurons instead of spawning isolated fragments.

items.title: Cross-Referenced Neurons
items.description: Every Neuron links bidirectionally to its source documents and to related Neurons. Follow a claim back to its origin, or forward to everything it touches — the associative trails Vannevar Bush imagined.

items.title: Instant Full-Text Search
items.description: Built-in SQLite FTS5 with auto-sync triggers keeps sources and compiled Neurons searchable in lockstep. No vector index to rebuild, no embedding drift.

items.title: Adapter Architecture
items.description: Storage, LLM, vision, and pipeline processors sit behind pluggable interfaces. Start local with SQLite and Claude Code; swap in R2, Anthropic API, or a CMS consumer (@webhouse/cms, Laravel) without touching the engine.

items.title: Extensible Ingestion
items.description: Connectors roadmap: Slack, Discord, Notion, GitHub, Linear. Today: Markdown, Web-Clipper, DOCX and PDF upload, plus native MCP for Claude Code and Cursor.

items.title: Curator, Not Dictator
items.description: The LLM proposes, you dispose. Twelve candidate kinds, an auto-approval threshold, and a replay-able event log keep every change reviewable and reversible.

data.title: Knowledge Infrastructure for the AI Era
data.metaDescription: Trail is a knowledge infrastructure engine that turns your sources into a persistent, compounding wiki — maintained by an LLM on your behalf. Realizing Vannevar Bush's 1945 Memex vision with modern LLMs.

sections.description: A minimalist engine to ingest, connect, and retrieve your organization's collective intelligence. Reimagining Vannevar Bush's 1945 vision with geometric precision.

sections.title: Architecture of thought.
sections.description: memx provides the primitives required to build complex semantic search, retrieval augmented generation pipelines, and interconnected knowledge bases.

items.title: Semantic Graph
items.description: Entities are linked not just by reference, but by semantic relationship. Traverse the graph to uncover implicit connections.

items.title: Local-First Sync
items.description: Built on CRDTs. Your knowledge graph lives locally for zero-latency access, syncing securely to the cloud when connected.

items.title: Vector Embeddings
items.description: Native integration with modern embedding models. Search by concept, not just keyword, across all ingested nodes.

items.title: Extensible Ingestion
items.description: Connectors for Slack, Notion, GitHub, and Linear. Automatically map scattered data into a unified cognitive structure.

items.title: Type-Safe API
items.description: Interact with your infrastructure via a strictly typed GraphQL and REST API. Predictable responses for resilient applications.

items.title: End-to-End Encryption
items.description: Your data is encrypted at rest and in transit. Only you hold the keys to decipher the nodes within your organizational graph.
